docs.title: DriConfig
docs.text:
A CLI to convert dbt models to PDFs.

Documentation: https://dribia.github.io/dbt2pdf

Source Code: https://github.com/dribia/dbt2pdf

dbt2PDF provides a command-line interface (CLI) to convert dbt models to PDF files.

docs.title: Key features
docs.text:
• CLI: An easy to use command-line interface (CLI) that can be used to convert dbt models to PDF files.
  • Customizable: Allows you to customize certain aspects of the output file.
  • Easy to use: Easy to use and can be integrated into your dbt workflow.
  • Open source: Open source and free to use.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: `commandline dbt2pdf generate \ --manifest-path ./manifest.json \ --title "DBT Documentation" \ --add-author john@example.com \ --add-author doe@example.com \ output.pdf

docs.title: Contribute
docs.text: Contributions to Dribia libraries are always welcome!

docs.title: Issues
docs.text:
Questions, feature requests and bug reports are all welcome as discussions or issues.

Please note which version of the library are you using whenever reporting a bug. 

python -c "import dbt2pdf; print(dbt2pdf.__version__)"

It would be very useful too to know which OS and Python version are your running dbt2PDF from.

docs.title: Contribute
docs.text:
In order to contribute, the first step is to clone yourself the code: repository: 

git clone https://github.com/dribia/dbt2pdf.git

Poetry is the best way to interact with this project, to install it, follow the official Poetry installation guide. With poetry installed, one can install the project dependencies with: 

poetry install

Now you should be ready to start coding and prepare your pull request.

Warning

Please, make sure to run:

  1. The project unit tests 
    make test-unit
  2. The linters (ruff and mypy): 
    make lint
  3. Apply all code formatting: 
    make format

before submitting your pull request.

Happy coding! 🎉

docs.title: PIP
docs.text:
Installation is as simple as: 

pip install dbt2pdf
dbt2PDF heavily depends on a Python library, that will be installed as dependencies:

  • fpdf2 is a library for simple & fast PDF document generation in Python.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
The dbt2pdf package provides a command-line interface (CLI) to convert dbt models to PDF files.

To view the available commands and full usage documentation, run: 

dbt2pdf --help

To view a given command usage documentation, the help flag can be used: 

dbt2pdf <command> --help

The only command available is generate, which converts dbt models to PDF files.

docs.title: Generate PDF files
docs.text:
To generate PDF files from dbt models, you can use the generate command: 

dbt2pdf generate

Warning

There is a required argument which is the destination path to save the PDF files:

dbt2pdf generate path/to/save/output.pdf

There are many options available to customize the PDF files generated. The following sections describe the available options.

docs.title: Define the dbt manifest file path
docs.text:
Warning

This is a required option. If not provided, the command will fail.

To define the dbt manifest file path, you can use the manifest-path option: 

dbt2pdf generate --manifest-path "path/to/manifest.json"

docs.title: Set title for the PDF
docs.text:
To set the title for the PDF, you can use the title option: 

dbt2pdf generate --title "My Title for dbt Documentation"

Note

The default title is "DBT Documentation".

docs.title: Add authors
docs.text:
To add authors to the documentation, you can use the add-author option: 

dbt2pdf generate --add-author "Jane Doe <jane@example.com>"

You can add multiple authors by using the add-author option multiple times: 

dbt2pdf generate \
 --add-author "Jane Doe <jane@example.com>"
 --add-author "John Doe <john@example.com>"

docs.title: Include macros in the documentation
docs.text:
To include macros in the documentation, you can use the add-macros-package option: 

dbt2pdf generate --add-macros-package "my_macros"

docs.title: Include logos
docs.text:
It is possible to include up to two logos in the documentation. To include a logo, you can use the add-logo option: 

dbt2pdf generate --add-logo "path/to/logo.png"

To include a second logo, you can use the add-logo option multiple times: 

dbt2pdf generate \
 --add-logo "path/to/logo1.png"
 --add-logo "path/to/logo2.png"
